title: Brachiaria orthostachys
intro: Brachiaria orthostachys, also known as Orthostachys brachiaria, is a species of plant in the Poaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Brachiaria orthostachys include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are employed to relieve colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Brachiaria orthostachys is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification places it within the order Poales. Further, it is a member of the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes various species of grasses. Brachiaria orthostachys is also classified as a species of the genus Brachiaria. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other grasses.
